reasoning: The market was at 19% on December 25, 2025. Since then, intense diplomatic efforts led by the US (Trump administration), Ukraine, and the EU have produced a "90% ready" 20-point peace plan and proposed a 60-day ceasefire. However, this progress is entirely one-sided. By early January 2026, Russia explicitly rejected the 60-day ceasefire (with aide Yuri Ushakov claiming it would "prolong the war"), demanded full territorial concessions in the Donbas, and escalated military strikes on Ukraine following an alleged drone attack on Vladimir Putin's residence. Russian officials have reportedly "largely withdrawn" from the peace plan as of January 3, 2026. Given the very tight timeline (less than 3 months until March 31, 2026), these significant Russian rejections and recent diplomatic breakdowns make it highly unlikely that a formal, mutual ceasefire will be signed in time. The probability is therefore slightly lower than the late-December Polymarket estimate.
